explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text containing ordinal numbers, ranking phrases, or positional references** (such as "11th-best," "1st District," "top 20," "second largest," or explicit dates like "August 1st").

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across diverse domains: city/global rankings, military or political hierarchies (e.g., "Central Military Commission," "EU sanctions list"), legal or administrative orders ("1st offense," "deportation orders"), and cultural or event rankings ("less well-known meteor shower"). It activates in multiple languages (English, Arabic, Tamil, Italian, Swedish) and scripts, indicating it detects the ordinal/ranking pattern irrespective of language.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts discuss events, announcements, or general statements without ordinal positioning or explicit ranking (e.g., "calls for an inquiry," "filming damage," "discusses booster jabs"). They lack phrases like "top X," "first/second," or date-ordinal constructions ("December 1st") that consistently trigger the neuron.

4. **Closing interpretation**: This neuron detects linguistic markers of ordinality, sequence, or relative position within a list or hierarchy. It abstracts away from specific topics to fire on the structural presence of ranking information.

results.actual_text: in October rose on the first of the month and was one of the most well-known full moons of the year: The Harvest Moon. The Harvest Moon is the nickname
results.plain_text: Blue moon to shine with Mars, Jupiter and Saturn on Halloween  A rare full moon will rise for this year's Halloween giving trick-or-treaters an extra treat for the holiday. The Halloween full moon is also a blue moon.  The night sky will bring an added treat this Halloween as it features a spectacle that has not occurred in nearly two decades.  Halloween is shaping up much differently this year due to the coronavirus pandemic with some communities electing to cancel trick-or-treating to reduce the risk of the virus spreading from one household to another. But in neighborhoods where young masqueraders will be going door-to-door collecting candy, they will have a bright full moon to help light the way.  This won’t be the typical full moon, either -- it will be a blue moon.  The moon rises in the sky as seen through the Four Towers, or C.T.B.A. (Cuatro Torres Business Area) in Madrid, Spain, Monday, Aug. 11, 2014. (AP Photo/Daniel Ochoa de Olza)  Contrary to its name, a blue moon does not appear blue in color. It is simply the nickname given to the second full moon in a calendar month.  “The modern understanding of “Blue Moon” only took off in the 1980s. It was a result of a much earlier mistake printed in a 1946 issue of Sky & Telescope magazine, and since then, the term has gone viral in the media,” the Old Farmer’s Almanac explained.  The first full moon in October rose on the first of the month and was one of the most well-known full moons of the year: The Harvest Moon. The Harvest Moon is the nickname given to the full moon closest to the fall equinox and typically glows in September, but 2020 was one of the instances where it appeared in early October.  Most trick-or-treaters in the U.S. will be able to see the blue moon on Halloween night with mainly clear conditions in the forecast for the Northeast, Plains, Rockies and West Coast.  A veil of clouds may be too thick for folks elsewhere across the country and into Canada to see the bright moon after sunset on Oct. 31.  Blue moons like the one this month happen about once every two-and-a-half years, according to NASA. This irregularity is how the phrase “once in a blue moon” came to be.  Those who are superstitious may want to carry around a good luck charm on Oct. 31 and keep it handy with a Friday the 13th right around the corner in November.  Just how rare is it for a Full Moon to glow on Halloween?  The Halloween blue moon is being touted as a rare celestial event by some, leaving people to wonder how uncommon this event is compared to a typical blue moon.  “On average, the moon is full on Halloween every 19 years,” NASA said. “In fact, every Halloween full moon is also a Blue Moon.”  The last time that a full moon illuminated the sky on Halloween night was in 2001; however, clouds obscured the spooky sight for folks across the Northeast, the northern Plains and along the Interstate 5 corridor in the Pacific Northwest.  The GOES-12 weather satellite took this image a few hours before sunset on Oct. 31, 2001. A tropical depression can be seen over the western Caribbean Sea that would go on to become Hurricane Michelle. (NOAA)  Trick-or-treaters this year may have kids of their own the next time that a blue moon falls on Halloween, which will happen in 2039.  Where cloud-free conditions prevail, there will be plenty more to see in the sky than just the moon.  Three bright planets will be visible after nightfall, including Mars off to the right of the moon and Jupiter and Saturn side-by-side in the southern sky.  Setting up a telescope and getting a good look at these planets could be a fun night for families that are not planning to trick-or-treat this year due to the pandemic, although no telescope is required to see the trio of planets.  Even looking at the moon through a telescope or simple pair of binoculars can reveal the countless craters that speckle the surface of Earth’s natural satellite.  Another type of blue moon?  The second full moon in a calendar month is the most common definition for a blue moon, but there is another way for a full moon to qualify for this nickname.  Rather than looking at the number of full moons in a month, a blue moon can be found when looking at the number of full moons in a season. Typically, there will be three full moons every season, but that is not always the case.  “If a season instead has four full moons, then the third full moon (not the fourth) in the season may be called a Blue Moon,” the Old Farmer’s Almanac said.  Just like a calendrical blue moon, seasonal blue moons occur every few years with the next one set to rise on Aug. 22, 2021.  Since there are two types of blue moons, it is possible for there to be both a calendrical blue moon and a seasonal blue moon in the same year, but it will be another 17 years before stargazers get to see the celestial double feature.  Related:  Keep checking back on AccuWeather.com and stay tuned to the AccuWeather Network on DirecTV, Frontier and Verizon Fios. Report a Typo Weather News  Drone captures aerial footage of homes ripped apart by deadly Zeta  Houses were peeled apart by intense winds from Hurricane Zeta -- a storm that has left at least six dead and 2 million without power.  What?! Another tropical threat could be on tap soon  With Zeta barely in the rearview mirror, forecasters are busy looking ahead to other dangers that may lurk in the Atlantic basin, and they say more records could soon fall.  No more waiting around: AccuWeather has your Election Day forecast right here  Millions of Americans have braved the elements, waiting in lines for hours, to cast votes early. Those who are waiting for Election Day may be in luck weather-wise.  The best heated blankets to keep you warm  Using heated blankets is a good way to cut down on thermostat costs, and keep you warm as the weather turns cold.  Winterize Your Home in 5 Steps  We have a blueprint for winterizing your home for the cold months. Drive down energy costs while keeping you and your home warm.  AccuWeather School: Why are city alleyways so windy?  If you live in or have been to a city, you have probably felt a gust of wind when you passed by an alleyway between two big buildings. Why does that happen? Let’s grab some building blocks to find out.

results.actual_text: college professors. Somebody get me on TV to tell the truth about the fraudulent credentials conferred on educational administrators and how they abuse the system. Ian Kay Wingo Way Mount
results.plain_text: It’s very hard to watch seemingly educated journalists pontificate about matters they obviously have no expertise in, while acting surprised at the level of corruption within the educational system. As a teacher of 28 years who wrote a book about endemic corruption within the educational system, I can’t get a word in edgewise despite many attempts to get the story out there. I am telling you that what you are seeing on the news is the tip of the iceberg. It’s very frustrating watching journalists rationalize the actions of a few Hollywood types, along with the CEOs of major corporations, who are taking heat for abuses when there are thousands, perhaps millions, of cases of this kind. And it has gone on for ages. But what do I know? I’m just the guy who uncovered the corruption and wrote the book. From listening to the commentary, you would think journalists didn’t know grade inflation and bribery were standard operating procedure for securing prestigious college slots. The perils of the mounting national debt keeps coming up and how student debt is a major factor, but there is no talk about the lack of productivity among college professors. Somebody get me on TV to tell the truth about the fraudulent credentials conferred on educational administrators and how they abuse the system. Ian Kay Wingo Way Mount Pleasant

It’s very frustrating watching journalists rationalize the actions of a few Hollywood types, along with the CEOs of major corporations, who are taking heat for abuses when there are thousands, perhaps millions, of cases of this kind.
predictions.reasoning: The phrase 'tip of the iceberg' uses 'tip' as a positional reference to the top, which may activate the neuron's sensitivity to ranking phrases.

results.actual_text: 022 gradually resumed the globe-trotting that once defined China’s best-known entrepreneur. He spent time in Tokyo and the Japanese countryside before making his way to
results.plain_text: (Bloomberg) -- Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. co-founder Jack Ma is in Hong Kong for a series of meetings with tech and finance executives, sustaining a recent flurry of activity that’s taken him round the world in the span of months. Most Read from Bloomberg The billionaire, who’s kept a low profile since Beijing in 2020 singled out first Ant Group Co. and then Alibaba for regulatory overhauls, has been meeting prominent figures based in the city, the Hong Kong Economic Times reported, citing anonymous sources. Ma arrived in past days but his exact agenda remains unclear, a person familiar with situation said, asking to remain anonymous discussing private arrangements. Ma has since late 2022 gradually resumed the globe-trotting that once defined China’s best-known entrepreneur. He spent time in Tokyo and the Japanese countryside before making his way to Thailand, where he toured foodie spots and reportedly took in a Muay Thai boxing match. Before that, he visited the US and Israel, the Financial Times reported in November. The executive — among China’s most recognizable faces — has been on the move around the time signs emerged that Beijing was relenting in a campaign to curtail the influence and power of internet giants. This month, Ma ceded controlling rights to his Ant fintech empire, which many observers took as a signal that punishing crackdown was nearing an end. Read more: Billionaire Jack Ma Ceding Ant Control Amid Firm’s Overhaul Ma mostly disappeared from public view after giving a 2020 speech that criticized Chinese regulators — right before Beijing scuttled what would have been a record Ant IPO. It triggered a series or regulatory probes and actions that targeted tech giants from Alibaba to Didi Global Inc., wiped out growth at rivals such as Tencent Holdings Ltd., and forced private businesspeople to curtail their activities. Many of Ma’s peers have since relinquished their formal corporate roles and increased donations to charity to align with President Xi Jinping’s vision of achieving “common prosperity.” Read more: Alibaba Founder Jack Ma Living in Tokyo for Half a Year, FT Says --With assistance from Dominic Lau. Most Read from Bloomberg Businessweek ©2023 Bloomberg L.P.

predictions.text: The billionaire, who’s kept a low profile since Beijing in 2020 singled out first Ant Group Co. and then Alibaba for regulatory overhauls, has been meeting prominent figures based in the city, the Hong Kong Economic Times reported, citing anonymous sources.
predictions.reasoning: The words 'first' and 'then' explicitly indicate an ordinal sequence, which strongly matches the neuron's pattern for ordering.

predictions.text: Ma has since late 2022 gradually resumed the globe-trotting that once defined China’s best-known entrepreneur.
predictions.reasoning: The term 'best-known' is a superlative ranking phrase, indicating highest recognition, aligning with the neuron's detection of ranking information.

predictions.text: Ma mostly disappeared from public view after giving a 2020 speech that criticized Chinese regulators — right before Beijing scuttled what would have been a record Ant IPO.
predictions.reasoning: The word 'record' implies a top ranking or highest level, which fits the neuron's sensitivity to ranking phrases.

results.actual_text: 6 tips para cuidar tu piel en el verano Síguenos en Facebook
results.plain_text: 6 tips para cuidar tu piel en el verano Síguenos en Facebook El verano ya llegó y muchas familias irás a las playas o diferentes lugares turísticos para disfrutar de las vacaciones de los pequeños de la casa y pasar un momento agradable en familia o con amigos, pero el desconocimiento sobre los cuidados que deberían tener, podrían causar graves problemas de salud. Es así que en el marco de la campaña “Prevención 365”, la Lic. Catya López, Especialista en prevención y Detección de la Liga Contra el Cáncer, brinda seis consejos para proteger la piel durante en verano. 1.- usar bloqueador solar No importa si solamente saldrás por 30 minutos, siempre debes usar bloqueador. Asimismo, debe ser reaplicado cada 4 horas. Este no puede ser menor de 30 y debe retocarse inmediatamente al salir del agua. 2. Usa prendas protectoras Aunque nos encontramos en verano, se recomienda usar sombreros de ala ancha, sombrillas, lentes con protección UV y camisetas anchas de manga larga que protejan a la piel de los peligros del sol. 3.- alimentos saludables Tal vez no lo sabías, pero consumir alimentos ricos en agua, vitaminas, sales minerales y sustancias antioxidantes, ayuda a mantener una piel saludable y protegerla de los efectos negativos del sol durante el verano. 4.- realízate despistajes preventivos Todas las personas, al menos una vez al año, deben acudir a un especialista para realizarse un despistaje preventivo de cáncer de piel. La Liga Contra el Cáncer con el apoyo de Unique y laboratorios Bristol Myers, realizará una campaña de despistajes del 04 al 20 de enero en sus 3 sedes en Lima por una donación de 20 soles. 5.-alerta a cualquier lunar Se recomienda hacerse regularmente una auto inspección de los lunares y manchas en la piel. Recuerda siempre que un lunar saludable es: proporcionado, regular, de color uniforme, menor de 6 milímetros y no causa molestias en la piel. 6. evita mucha exposición al sol Está comprobado que entre las 10 de la mañana y las 3 de la tarde hay mayor radiación ultravioleta y tu piel se podría ver afectada.

predictions.text: 1.- usar bloqueador solar No importa si solamente saldrás por 30 minutos, siempre debes usar bloqueador. Asimismo, debe ser reaplicado cada 4 horas. Este no puede ser menor de 30 y debe retocarse inmediatamente al salir del agua.
predictions.reasoning: The section begins with the ordinal number '1.-' and includes numerical references like '30 minutos' and '4 horas', which are typical triggers for the neuron.

La Liga Contra el Cáncer con el apoyo de Unique y laboratorios Bristol Myers, realizará una campaña de despistajes del 04 al 20 de enero en sus 3 sedes en Lima por una donación de 20 soles.
